summary.intent: Rebase PR #2587 (GitHub App authentication support) onto the latest main branch to resolve merge conflicts and update the PR.
summary.outcome: Successfully rebased the PR, resolved conflicts in build_fbc.py and build_microshift.py, then discovered and fixed a bug where local git operations were falling back to GITHUB_TOKEN instead of using GitHub App auth due to calling get_github_git_auth_env() without a URL.

repo: art-tools uses GitHub App authentication with 4 installations across orgs: operator-framework, openshift-priv, openshift, openshift-eng
get_github_client_for_org(org) is the primary authenticated client factory; get_github_git_auth_env(url) needs a URL to determine which installation to use
When multiple installations exist and no URL is provided, the auth helper refuses to auto-pick and falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN (PAT)
Jenkins CI jobs consume GitHub App credentials from Jenkins secrets and reveal runtime auth behavior in console logs

code.finding: add_all() was calling _git_env() without a URL, triggering GitHub App installation ambiguity and falling back to GITHUB_TOKEN for a purely local 'git add' operation that doesn't need any auth
commit_push() calls add_all() which creates its own auth env; local operations (add, status, commit) don't need GitHub auth, only git push does
Fix pattern: added _local_env() returning only GIT_NO_PROMPTS for local-only operations; use _git_env_for_remote(remote) for operations contacting remotes like fetch/push

workflow: Jenkins MCP server tools enable searching build console logs for specific patterns to diagnose runtime auth behavior
Rebase conflict resolution requires understanding both what main added (e.g., slack channel routing in build_fbc.py) and what the PR removed (GITHUB_TOKEN checks)
Production Jenkins logs reveal issues that unit tests miss - runtime auth fallback behavior only visible in CI console output

friction: Merge conflicts in build_fbc.py and build_microshift.py required careful manual resolution to preserve slack channel routing from main while keeping PR's auth changes
GITHUB_TOKEN fallback issue wasn't caught by tests - only discovered by manually inspecting Jenkins console logs after the fact
Ambiguous installation error message ('GitHub App has 4 installations') could be clearer about requiring explicit org or URL
Had to trace through multiple layers (Jenkins log → GitRepository → get_github_git_auth_env → _git_env) to find root cause of auth fallback
open_items: GIT_PASSWORD (GitHub App token) logging in plaintext tracked in separate PR #2694
CodeRabbit review comments about async/await patterns for PyGithub blocking calls still unaddressed
ci/prow/security test failure mentioned in PR - may need separate investigation
Multiple CodeRabbit suggestions around offloading PyGithub calls to asyncio.to_thread in various pipeline files
